title: Viborgia austriaca
intro: Viburnum austriacum, commonly known as Austrian cranberry, is a deciduous shrub in the Adoxaceae family, native to Europe and parts of Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help to soothe digestive issues and support the immune system. The bioactive constituents of Viburnum austriacum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Viburnum austriacum is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is used to treat various health issues, including fever, coughs, and gastrointestinal problems.
